title: Honeywell and TruAlt Bioenergy Sign Agreement to Produce 80,000 TPA of SAF in India
content: Honeywell and TruAlt Bioenergy Limited have signed a strategic agreement to establish one of India's first dedicated Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) production facilities. Utilizing Honeywellâ€™s Ethanol-to-Jet (ETJ) technology, the plant aims to produce 80,000 tons per annum (TPA) of SAF using ethanol derived from agricultural sources. This partnership aligns with Indiaâ€™s "Aatmanirbhar Bharat" vision by fostering domestic biofuel production and supporting the decarbonization of the aviation sector. The process converts renewable ethanol into a cost-effective, cleaner fuel that can also yield renewable diesel for road transport. TruAlt Managing Director Vijay Nirani highlighted that this initiative connects Indian farmers to the global aviation decarbonization agenda, creating economic value while reducing reliance on external energy sources.
author: Analysts Account
